sentenceStructure.paragraphPattern: Complex but clear compound sentences linking cause and effect. Active voice. Calibration clauses that add context about when advice applies.

rhetoric.hookPattern: Contrarian stance opener - challenges conventional management or marketing wisdom
rhetoric.ctaStyle: Soft CTA - 'Hope you tune in' style, never aggressive

canonicalExamples.text: I think efficient and strategic project management precludes the need for check-ins.
canonicalExamples.why: Contrarian stance opener that challenges conventional management wisdom with operational insight

canonicalExamples.text:
I think efficient and strategic project management precludes the need for check-ins. If everything is set up, then the work should be completed in the timeline set forth.

With freelancers, the guardrails I implement depend entirely on the level of expertise the freelancer has. With an experienced content or design marketer, I let them have at it, and give a basic schema of project scope.
canonicalExamples.why: Complete demonstration of contrarian opener, systems thinking, expertise calibration, and specific PM terminology

canonicalExamples.text:
Speaking with the Trellis team tomorrow on some of the best strategies to employ for an Amazon-first business! Hope you tune in!

https://lnkd.in/eysVWTi4
canonicalExamples.why: Event promotion showing efficient communication style - activity-first, value proposition, soft CTA, clean format

canonicalExamples.text: When deliverables are delayed, usually that's stated in a sprint recap meeting, with an updated due date.
canonicalExamples.why: Systems-first observation opener that frames the process before addressing the human element
Soft, non-aggressive call to action consistent with her efficient communication style

openingPatterns.pattern: Opens by challenging conventional management or marketing wisdom. Takes a position others might shy away from.
examples: I think efficient and strategic project management precludes the need for check-ins.
The best consumer growth leaders aren't just marketers or just operators.

openingPatterns.pattern: Opens with a process or structural insight that frames everything that follows. Positions as architect, not participant.
examples: With freelancers, the guardrails I implement depend entirely on the level of expertise the freelancer has.
Managing one brand is marketing. Managing a portfolio is operations.
If everything is set up, then the work should be completed in the timeline set forth.

openingPatterns.pattern: Activity-first framing for event promotion. Direct, efficient, value-focused. No preamble.
examples: Speaking with the Trellis team tomorrow on some of the best strategies to employ for an Amazon-first business!

placementRules: Default: No emojis in professional content
Exception: Occasional single emoji for visual break (rare)
Substitute: Uses exclamation marks sparingly for genuine enthusiasm
Philosophy: Let the words carry the weight

structuralPatterns.name: Contrarian Stance -> Explanation -> Calibration
structure: Calibration by context - 'depends on...' or 'for X vs Y...'
structuralPatterns.useCase: Management insights, operational content, thought leadership

structuralPatterns.name: Announcement -> Value Prop -> Soft CTA -> Link
structure: Topic/value proposition - what audience will learn

formattingToAvoid: Em dashes - prefers periods or commas for cleaner flow
Heavy emoji usage - maintains professional, text-first aesthetic
Excessive hashtags - clean posts preferred (sample had zero)
Rhetorical questions as openers - leads with statements, not questions
Overly formatted posts - no excessive headers, bold, or structure
All-caps emphasis - calm authority, not shouting
toneToAvoid: Hustle culture glorification - believes in systems, not working harder
Overly promotional language - value-first, not sales-first
Humble-bragging - direct about achievements when relevant
Academic/theoretical voice - speaks from operator seat, not professor seat

hashtagStyle.placement: End of post if used at all; default is no hashtags

taggingStyle.frequency: Rare - used only when directly relevant
patterns: Company mentions by name, not @tag ('Trellis team' not '@Trellis')
Low tagging frequency - clean, uncluttered posts
Partner references are professional, acknowledging collaboration
